title: Getter returns direct reference to internal array
problem: getAsymAlgAbility() returns direct reference to internal array without defensive copying. This allows external code to modify the internal state, potentially violating immutability expectations and causing security issues.

title: Getter returns direct reference to private key material
problem: getD() returns direct reference to private exponent without defensive copying. This is highly sensitive cryptographic material.

title: Getter returns direct reference to prime factors
problem: getPrime() returns direct reference to prime factors [p, q] without defensive copying. These are highly sensitive RSA private key components.

title: Getter returns direct reference to CRT exponents
problem: getPexp() returns direct reference to CRT exponents [dp, dq] without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to CRT coefficient
problem: getCoef() returns direct reference to CRT coefficient without defensive copying.

title: Default constructor leaves asymAlgAbility uninitialized
problem: The default constructor no longer initializes asymAlgAbility array, leaving it as null. Callers using getAsymAlgAbility() will receive null, potentially causing NullPointerException.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getX() returns direct reference to internal byte array without defensive copying. For cryptographic objects, this allows callers to modify the key material directly, which is a security vulnerability.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getY() returns direct reference to internal byte array without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getM() returns direct reference to internal byte array without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getC() returns direct reference to internal byte array without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getK() returns direct reference to private key material without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getX() returns direct reference to public key material without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getY() returns direct reference to public key material without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getR() returns direct reference to signature value without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to internal byte array
problem: getS() returns direct reference to signature value without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to key material
problem: getM() returns direct reference to modulus without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to key material
problem: getE() returns direct reference to public exponent without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to key material
problem: getM() returns direct reference to modulus without defensive copying.

title: Getter returns direct reference to key material
problem: getE() returns direct reference to public exponent without defensive copying.

title: Inconsistent validation between constructor and setter
problem: The constructor validates that l <= c.length, but the setC() setter doesn't perform this validation. This allows setting a ciphertext array that is smaller than the l value, creating an inconsistent state.

title: Array length check doesn't prevent buffer overflow
problem: The check `if (len > RSAref_MAX_LEN)` throws an exception, but when GetObjectField returns null, the code continues without initializing native_key->m. The subsequent GetByteArrayRegion call with a null m_array would crash.

title: Missing null check for exponent array
problem: Similar to the modulus case, if e_array is null, the code silently continues without initializing native_key->e.

title: Missing null checks for signature r and s arrays
problem: The java_to_native_ECCSignature function doesn't check if r_array or s_array are null before calling GetByteArrayRegion.

title: Missing null check for ECCPrivateKey k array
problem: The java_to_native_ECCPrivateKey function doesn't check if k_array is null before calling GetByteArrayRegion.

title: bytesToHex displays entire array without truncation
problem: The bytesToHex method now displays the entire byte array without any truncation. For large keys (512-bit ECC), this will output 128 hex characters per coordinate, which could overwhelm logs.

title: Missing validation in setCtM setter
problem: The setCtM() setter lacks null validation, while setCtS() has it. This inconsistency could lead to unexpected behavior.

title: Missing validation in setSigM setter
problem: The setSigM() setter lacks null validation.

title: No error handling when ecc_cipher_obj is null
problem: If native_to_java_ECCCipher returns null, the code continues and creates a HybridCipher object with a null ctS field.
